Bus Timings

Abu Dhabi A10 Bus Timetable - MBZ City Bus Station to Abu Dhabi Airport ECC

March 3, 2026
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Among its key routes, the A10 bus links the Mohamed Bin Zayed City Bus Station to Abu Dhabi Airport ECC, catering to both travelers and local residents.

Operational Details

The A10 bus operates throughout the day, ensuring reliable service for commuters and travelers heading to the airport. Below are the key timings for the A10 departing from both MBZ City Bus Station and Abu Dhabi Airport ECC:

From MBZ City Bus Station From Abu Dhabi Airport ECC
05:02 06:30
06:02 07:30
07:01 08:30
08:04 09:30
09:02 10:30
10:06 11:30
11:04 12:30
12:03 13:30
13:04 14:30
14:03 15:30
15:03 16:30
16:03 17:27
17:03 18:30
18:03 19:30
19:02 20:30
20:03 21:30
21:03 22:30
22:04 23:30

Bus A10 Timetable

For a detailed schedule, you can check the official AD Mobility website or follow this link for A10 Bus Timings.

Ticket Pricing: Passengers can conveniently pay for A10 bus trips using the Hafilat Card, Abu Dhabi’s cashless payment system. The fare for travel within one zone is AED 2, with an additional charge of AED 0.05 per kilometer for longer journeys. Hafilat Cards can be easily topped up online or at kiosks throughout Abu Dhabi, making the travel experience smooth and efficient.

Route Overview

The A10 bus route travels through a variety of significant locations, making it an essential service for those commuting between Mohamed Bin Zayed City and Abu Dhabi Airport. Below is the full list of stops along the route:

  1. Musaffah The starting point, serving a bustling industrial area.

  2. Mohamed Bin Zayed City Bus Station A major transit hub for commuters.

  3. Al Falah Academy An educational institution catering to local students.

  4. Al Rasi Street A busy thoroughfare providing access to various amenities.

  5. Emirates Heritage Club A cultural landmark showcasing the UAE’s heritage.

  6. Naseem Al Barr Street A residential area with local conveniences.

  7. Al Dolpheen Street Another residential stop for the community.

  8. Al Widad Street A quiet street offering local access.

  9. Mubarak Bin Sumaida Mosque A prominent place of worship.

  10. LLH Medical Centre A healthcare facility serving the local population.

  11. Al Ain University A key educational institution offering higher education.

  12. Burjeel Medical City A comprehensive medical facility.

  13. Bohra Islamic Cultural Complex A center for cultural and religious activities.

  14. Mazyad Mall A shopping destination for residents and visitors.

  15. Capital Mall Another popular shopping center along the route.

  16. Abu Dhar Al-Afary Mosque A spiritual landmark for the community.

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Najm Suhayl Street A road connecting to various neighborhoods.

  2. Al Maqta Bus Interchange · A A significant transfer point for bus connections.

  3. Wahat Al Karamah A memorial honoring UAE martyrs.

  4. Abu Dhabi Airport Terminal A Departure The bus stop for terminal A.

  5. Abu Dhabi Airport ECC The final stop, providing access to the airport.

Some other Abu Dhabi bus routes you can explore include the 222 bus route, which goes from Osama Bin Zaid Mosque to Hili Street, and the B45 route, which runs from Prison to Bawabat Al Sharq Mall North.

Accessibility and Services

The A10 bus is designed to accommodate passengers with special needs, ensuring an inclusive travel experience. The buses feature:

  • Ramps for wheelchair access.

  • Designated seating for individuals with mobility challenges.
  • Audio-visual aids for passengers with hearing or vision impairments.

This commitment to accessibility makes the A10 a suitable option for all commuters.

Nearby Attractions and Landmarks

Throughout the journey, passengers on the A10 bus can explore several notable attractions and landmarks:

  1. Emirates Heritage Club A cultural hub where visitors can learn about the UAE’s traditions and heritage.

  2. Mazyad Mall and Capital Mall Popular shopping destinations offering a variety of retail and dining options.

  3. Wahat Al Karamah A beautiful memorial that pays tribute to the sacrifices made by Emirati soldiers, providing a reflective space for visitors.

Journey Experience

Taking the A10 bus from MBZ City Bus Station to Abu Dhabi Airport ECC offers a convenient journey through diverse sights. Starting in Musaffah, known for its industrial significance, the trip moves through Mohamed Bin Zayed City, showcasing residential neighborhoods and local amenities.

The bus also stops at popular shopping destinations such as Mazyad Mall and Capital Mall, ideal for last-minute retail therapy. Culminating at Abu Dhabi Airport ECC, this route provides practical transportation while offering glimpses of the city’s vibrant mix of modernity and tradition.

Travel Tips for Bus A10

  • Plan Ahead: Check the bus timings to avoid unnecessary waiting, especially during peak hours.

  • Use the Hafilat Smart Card: This allows for quick, cashless payment on all Abu Dhabi buses.
  • Monitor Bus Updates: Use the AD Mobility app for real-time updates and schedules to optimize your journey.
  • Avoid Peak Times: If you prefer a quieter ride, avoid traveling during the busiest periods in the morning and afternoon.

The A10 bus provides a dependable and efficient route between Mohamed Bin Zayed City Bus Station and Abu Dhabi Airport ECC.

Whether you’re commuting to work or catching a flight, this bus guarantees a smooth and cost-effective journey through Abu Dhabi. Step onto the A10 and enjoy the city’s vibrant mix of culture and convenience! Don’t forget to check out the complete list of bus routes in Abu Dhabi for more travel possibilities!

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: March 3, 2026.

Written by Al Khaleej
